title: Google Project Management Certificate
description: Title slide introducing the full study guide for the Google PM Certificate.

title: What Is Project Management?
description: Definition of project management and its key components.

title: What Does a Project Manager Do?
description: The day-to-day responsibilities and focus areas of a project manager.

title: The Value of a Project Manager
description: How project managers add value through prioritization, delegation, and communication.

title: Professional Project Management Skills
description: Core competencies and soft skills that make project managers effective.

title: Project Structures and Life Cycles
description: The four phases of the project life cycle: Initiation, Planning, Execution, Closing.

title: Organizational Structure and Company Culture
description: Classic, matrix, and project-based org structures and why culture matters.

title: How Company Culture Affects Project Management
description: How organizational culture influences decisions, communication, and PM effectiveness.

title: Key Parts of Project Initiation
description: Section divider — Initiation. Goals, scope, deliverables, stakeholders, and resources.

title: Setting SMART Goals
description: The SMART framework for setting Specific, Measurable, Attainable, Relevant, Time-bound goals.

title: Scope and Scope Creep
description: Defining project scope and preventing scope creep with clear documentation and change control.

title: Communicating and Working with Stakeholders
description: Identifying stakeholders, the RACI chart, and stakeholder management steps.

title: The Benefits of Project Planning
description: Why planning matters and what goes into a project plan.

title: Project Planning for Beginners
description: The five core elements: tasks, milestones, people, documentation, and time.

title: Building a Project Plan
description: Step-by-step approach to building a project plan using a spreadsheet.

title: Negotiating with Stakeholders
description: Key negotiation principles and using empathy to find win-win solutions.

title: How to Create a Project Budget
description: Budget components and step-by-step budgeting process.

title: Risk Management Basics
description: Risk management process and types of project risks.

title: Resources and Tools to Manage Your Project
description: Project resources: budget, people, materials, and common PM tools.

title: The Project Communication Plan
description: What a communication plan outlines and why communication plans prevent failure.

title: Communicate Effectively as a Project Manager
description: Section divider — Execution. 90% of PM time is communication.

title: How to Run a Successful Meeting
description: Characteristics of effective meetings: structured, intentional, collaborative, inclusive.

title: How to Organize Effective Meetings
description: Before, during, and after a meeting best practices.

title: Project Tracking Methods
description: Gantt charts, Kanban boards, burndown charts, and status reports.

title: Effective Project Management During Execution
description: PM focus areas during execution and the project plan as a living document.

title: Leadership vs Management in a Successful Team
description: The distinction between management and leadership, and what makes a team.

title: How Project Managers Improve Team Effectiveness
description: What strong teams share and how PMs build team effectiveness.

title: How Project Managers Can Influence Those Around Them
description: Four steps for influencing without authority: credibility, framing, evidence, emotion.

title: How Project Managers Can Build Customer Satisfaction
description: Relationship strategies and soft skills for building customer trust.

title: Quality Management and Continuous Improvement
description: Defining quality, the triple constraint, and continuous improvement.

title: Understanding Change Management
description: Change management process and core concepts including ownership and urgency.

title: Closing a Project
description: Five steps to properly close a project: completion, acceptance, retrospective, archive, celebrate.

title: Introduction to Agile
description: Section divider — Agile & Scrum. Agile's four characteristics: iterative, flexible, collaborative, customer-focused.

title: The Agile Manifesto
description: Four core values of the Agile Manifesto.

title: Agile vs. Waterfall
description: Side-by-side comparison of Agile and Waterfall methodologies.

title: Understanding Scrum
description: Scrum definition, three pillars (transparency, inspection, adaptation), and five core values.

title: The Scrum Team
description: Three Scrum roles: Product Owner, Scrum Master, and Development Team.

title: The Product Backlog
description: What the product backlog is, user stories, and backlog refinement.

title: The Sprint
description: Four sprint ceremonies: Sprint Planning, Daily Standup, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective.

title: Scrum Artifacts
description: Three Scrum artifacts: Product Backlog, Sprint Backlog, and Increment.

title: Velocity and Estimation
description: Velocity, story points, Fibonacci scale, and planning poker.

title: The Definition of Done
description: Shared criteria for when a work item is truly complete.

title: The Scrum Master Role in Detail
description: How the Scrum Master serves the team, Product Owner, and organization.

title: Applying Agile in Real Environments
description: Challenges of real-world Agile adoption and strategies for success.

title: Agile Beyond Software: The Broader Application
description: Industries using Agile and universal Agile principles that apply anywhere.

title: How to Maximize the Value of a Scrum Team
description: Team health practices and common Scrum pitfalls to avoid.

title: Scaled Agile and Hybrid Approaches
description: SAFe for multiple Scrum teams and hybrid Agile/Waterfall approaches.

title: Agile Mindset vs. Waterfall Thinking
description: Contrasting Waterfall assumptions with Agile mindset beliefs.

title: Kanban and Other Agile Frameworks
description: Overview of Kanban, XP (Extreme Programming), and Lean frameworks.

title: Delivering Value with Agile
description: Continuous delivery model and key Agile value metrics.

title: Agile Leadership and Servant Leadership
description: Servant leadership in Agile and behaviors that enable team success.

title: Sprint Retrospective in Practice
description: Three retrospective questions and rules for effective retrospectives.

title: OKRs: Objectives and Key Results
description: OKR framework with inspiring objectives and quantitative key results.

title: Data-Driven Decision Making in Agile
description: Key Agile metrics and best practices for using data well.

title: Agile and Scrum: Key Takeaways
description: Four key takeaways: Agile is a mindset, Scrum is a framework, people first, inspect and adapt.

title: Building a Career in Project Management
description: Section divider — Careers. High demand, good pay, and clear career growth path.

title: Finding a Job in Project Management
description: Entry-level roles, how to stand out, and the STAR interview method.

title: Developing a Personal Career Plan
description: Short-term, mid-term, and long-term PM career goals.

title: Building Your Professional Network
description: Where and how to network effectively in the PM community.

title: You Are Ready to Manage Projects
description: Closing slide summarizing all six sections of the Google PM Certificate.
